result:
1. Turn switch A on and leave it on for about 10 minutes.
2. Turn switch A off.
3. Turn switch B on.
4. Immediately enter the room.
5. The bulb that is lit is controlled by B.
6. Of the two unlit bulbs, touch each one; the warm one is controlled by A and the cool one is controlled by C.

Why this works: Each switch leaves a distinct, observable signature on its bulb. B's bulb is the only one currently powered, so it is the lit one. A was on long enough to heat its bulb's filament and glass but is now off, so its bulb is dark yet warm. C was never switched on, so its bulb is dark and at room temperature. Sight distinguishes lit from unlit, and touch distinguishes the two unlit bulbs, giving a unique identification for all three on a single visit.
